summary.intent: Implement all remaining stories (2.2–2.4, 3.1–3.3, 4.1–4.3) of a BTC metrics dashboard using a coordinated dev team of 3 senior developers, 3 code reviewers, and a devil's advocate, all following the bmad workflow.
summary.outcome: All 9 planned stories plus a post-sprint Story 4.4 (quality fixes) were implemented and reviewed. 105 tests pass, build is clean, and all 4 epics are marked done in sprint-status.yaml.

repo: No barrel files (index.ts) anywhere in src/ — all imports go directly to specific modules
Unidirectional data flow strictly enforced: only services write to nanostores, components only subscribe/read
Services initialized in Layout.astro <script> block: ConnectionManager, MarketDataService, and tab-title-updater all bootstrapped there
format-utils is the single source of all number/price formatting — no inline Intl.NumberFormat anywhere in components
connectionManager uses singleton pattern; MarketDataService uses singleton pattern
chartStore uses full-replace strategy on each 60s poll (not incremental append) — acceptable for v1
statusStore initializes to 'connecting' (not 'live') — static HTML shells must match this initial state
PriceCard uses fixed h-[42px] sm:h-[56px] (not min-h) for CLS=0 compliance, derived from 36px*1.1 and 48px*1.1 font metrics
Backoff sequence: 1s→2s→4s→8s→16s→30s cap; MAX_BINANCE_RETRIES=3, MAX_COINCAP_RETRIES=3 before REST fallback
CoinCap message format: { bitcoin: '68432.17' } — needs normalization to PriceTick
Promotion canary: checks Binance every 60s from CoinCap/REST mode, promotes on successful probe WS onopen

code.finding: isConnecting boolean flag added to guard against re-entrant connectBinance() calls during promotion race condition
connect() resets this.previousPrice = null so first tick after reconnect/promotion shows neutral direction instead of stale pre-disconnect direction

attemptPromotion() creates a probe WebSocket; on success calls connect() which calls cleanup() — probe WS is already closed at line 300 before this, so no orphan, but race with CoinCap onclose is possible
AbortController field added; both fetch() calls pass signal, stop() calls abort() to cancel in-flight requests
stop() clears timer AND aborts controller — full cleanup path
scheduleNext() clears existing timer before scheduling new one — prevents timer accumulation
Initial static HTML must show 'Connecting...' with amber dot to match statusStore initial value of 'connecting' — originally showed 'Live' causing a hydration flash
Stale badge uses visibility/opacity pattern (not display:none) for CLS=0; needs aria-hidden='true' when hidden, toggled to 'false' when .visible class is added
toChartData(points: ChartPoint[]) — parameter type annotation required; uses direct import from '../lib/types', not a barrel
priceStore.subscribe() return value (unsubscribe fn) is discarded — acceptable since page never unmounts, but inconsistent with architecture's cleanup emphasis

workflow: bmad flow for post-sprint defects: /bmad-create-story to produce a self-contained story file with acceptance criteria, then /bmad-dev-story + /bmad-code-review — story file is the single source of truth, not chat history
Story ordering matters for parallel dev teams: 4.1 and 4.2 depend on connection-manager.ts from 2.2 — out-of-order execution (4.3 first) was needed to unblock dev-carol while dev-alice finished 2.2

Devil's advocate runs after all code reviews pass, with access to the full codebase — most valuable for cross-cutting concerns (CLS, bundle budget, architecture rule compliance) that per-story reviewers miss
Deferred findings from one story review should be tracked as explicit action items for the story that owns them (e.g., previousPrice reset deferred from 2.2 review to 4.1 scope)
When static HTML shells are rendered before JS hydration, their initial state must match the store's initial value to avoid flash-of-incorrect-state
Agent shutdown should be dispatched in parallel for agents with no remaining dependencies — all 4 Epics 1-3 agents shut down simultaneously

friction: Story 4.1 was blocked by missing connection-manager.ts from Story 2.2 — parallel dev assignment required out-of-order resequencing (4.3 first)
Story 4.3 required adding stale badge to PriceCard even though PriceCard was Story 2.3's scope — ownership boundary was ambiguous (statusStore-driven = 4.x, but file = 2.x)
Devil's advocate was spawned before all implementations were complete — findings #1 and #2 were 'expected, in progress' rather than actionable, creating noise
dev-carol received a stale re-notification about stale badge work that was already completed, causing confusion
reviewer-2 received a redundant re-check request after already completing the AbortController verification, also causing confusion
idle_notification spam from multiple agents between task completions added noise to the conversation
open_items: Promotion canary WebSocket is not tracked in this.ws — low-probability race if disconnect() is called during the 5-second probe window (deferred per spec pattern, reviewer-3 noted)
Global document.querySelector('.status-dot') in StatusIndicator and similar selectors in other islands are architecturally brittle — would break if multiple component instances were ever rendered

nanostores subscribe() return values (unsubscribe fns) are discarded in tab-title-updater.ts and all Astro island script blocks — acceptable since Astro islands have no teardown lifecycle, but GC risk for 24h+ sessions if subscriber callbacks close over DOM elements
chartStore full-replace strategy works for v1 but doesn't support incremental append (shift-oldest) as the architecture spec implied for future modifications

Story 3.2 spec ambiguity: '24h Range' label placement inside opacity container means it hides with the data during loading — AC8 says labels should be visible when marketStore is null, but spec's own HTML contradicts this
